title: GH #27.3+4: budget hard clamps + guarded, audit-first reset-budget
action: Per D-GHF-C: 1) resolveCellBudgets (~:1117) — values must be finite integers >=1; clamp to hard max {max_claims:9, max_failed_attempts:12, max_same_signature:6}; non-integer/out-of-range falls back to default (resolve) and is REFUSED at authoring (validateNewCell: budgets, when present, must be a plain object whose keys are exactly the known three and whose values are integers in [1, hard max]). 2) resetCellBudget (~:1221) + handleCellsResetBudget (bee.mjs ~:927): refuse with typed error unless checkCellBudgets(cell) is currently not-ok (cell actually budget-blocked); require an actor — --operator flag or BEE_AGENT_NAME env, refuse when absent; write the audit logDecision BEFORE writeCell and include the actor in the decision text and a by_actor field in the budget_resets marker. GUARD ORDERING (plan-check constraint): keep the reason-check FIRST (:1222) and the not-found check (:1226) BEFORE the new actor/blocked guards, so existing error contracts hold. 3) MUST-UPDATE existing tests: test_lib.mjs:1125 (audited-reset case — supply an actor) and confirm :1148 error-precedence cases still pass unchanged (they must — do not alter their expected messages). Add new cases: clamp, authoring refusal, reset refused on healthy cell, reset refused without actor, decision-before-write ordering. Registry: cells.reset-budget parameters gain operator. Run onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply. Commit: fix(budget): hard clamps + guarded audit-first reset (GH #27) [ghf-5].

truths: no path raises a budget above hard max
reset-budget on a non-exhausted cell is a typed refusal
audit record exists even if the cell write crashes

trace.outcome: resolveCellBudgets clamps to a hard max (9/12/6, 3x default) and falls back to DEFAULT_BUDGETS on non-integer/below-floor declared values; validateNewCell refuses malformed/out-of-range budgets outright at authoring; resetCellBudget now refuses (typed RESET_NOT_NEEDED) unless checkCellBudgets reports not-ok, and refuses without an actor (--operator or BEE_AGENT_NAME), with the audit decision logged before the cell write so a failed write still leaves an audit trail.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": [
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs:1077-1330 (D2 cell-lifetime budgets block, incl. the pre-existing audited-reset case at ~1125 and the reason/not-found precedence case at ~1148)",
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs:493-527 (cells.reset-budget registry example), 2523-2620 (CLI end-to-end reset-budget + claim-exhaustion tests)"
  ],
  "tests_added_or_changed": [
    "test_lib.mjs: resolveCellBudgets clamp/default boundary test (999999->9, exactly-9 stays 9, 10->9, non-integer/0/negative/non-numeric -> DEFAULT)",
    "test_lib.mjs: addCell/validateNewCell authoring-refusal test (array budgets, unknown key, non-integer, over-hard-max, zero -- all refused; a valid at-hard-max object accepted verbatim)",
    "test_lib.mjs: resetCellBudget refused typed RESET_NOT_NEEDED on a healthy (non-budget-blocked) cell",
    "test_lib.mjs: resetCellBudget refused without an actor (message names both --operator and BEE_AGENT_NAME), and a separate test proving the BEE_AGENT_NAME env fallback supplies the actor when --operator is omitted",
    "test_lib.mjs: audit-before-write ordering test -- forces a writeCell failure via a read-only .bee/cells dir (chmod 555) and asserts the decision is still recorded and the cell file is byte-unchanged",
    "test_lib.mjs: updated the existing audited-reset case (~1125) to pass an --operator actor and assert by_actor is recorded; the reason/not-found precedence case (~1148) was left byte-unchanged and still passes",
    "test_bee_cli.mjs: updated the cells.reset-budget registry-example test to force demo-1 budget-blocked first (injected ledger entries) since the door now refuses on a healthy cell; the registry example itself gained --operator",
    "test_bee_cli.mjs: updated the CLI end-to-end reset-budget test (budget-cli-1) to actually exhaust the budget first and pass --operator; added a new no-actor CLI refusal test (budget-cli-1b, explicit env with BEE_AGENT_NAME stripped); added --operator to the budget-cli-2 claim-exhaustion test's reset-budget call"
  ],
  "red_failure_evidence": "Before this cell: resolveCellBudgets had no upper clamp (a declared budgets.max_claims of 999999 resolved un-clamped -- confirmed by reading cells.mjs:1145-1157 pre-change, which only defaulted on <=0/non-finite, never capped a large value), validateNewCell had no budgets validation at all (a malformed budgets object was accepted silently at cells.mjs:332-388 pre-change), and resetCellBudget had no exhaustion guard and an optional actor (cells.mjs:1256-1281 pre-change: readCell -> unconditionally append a budget_resets entry -> writeCell, decision logged AFTER the write, by_session only, no by_actor). Concretely reproduced pre-change: resolveCellBudgets({budgets:{max_claims:999999}}).max_claims === 999999 (no clamp); addCell(root, {..., budgets:{max_claims:0}}) succeeded (no refusal); resetCellBudget(root, freshCellId, 'any reason') succeeded on a cell with zero attempts (no blocked-check, no actor requirement). The new test suite's first run failed RED at import time -- 'SyntaxError: The requested module ../lib/cells.mjs does not provide an export named resolveCellBudgets' -- because resolveCellBudgets was not yet exported/hardened; after implementing the export + clamp + validateNewCell guard + resetCellBudget's actor/blocked guards + audit-before-write, the full suite went green: test_lib.mjs 406/406 (previously 400/400 pre-cell), test_bee_cli.mjs 211/211, test_lib_mirror.mjs 4/4 (mirrors synced via onboard_bee.mjs --apply).",
  "verification_run": "node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs -- all green (406/406, 211/211, mirror byte-identical)"
}
trace.verify_output: test_lib.mjs 406/406, test_bee_cli.mjs 211/211, test_lib_mirror.mjs 4/4 all green (mirrors synced via onboard_bee.mjs --apply)

checks.evidence: pick()=Math.min(value, BUDGET_HARD_MAX[key]) for valid int>=1; hard max {9,12,6}; authoring refuses >hardMax
validateNewCell cells.mjs:385-410 refuses non-object, unknown key, non-integer, out-of-range
actor operator||BEE_AGENT_NAME else throw; order reason->not-found->actor->blocked per constraint
logDecision with by_actor BEFORE writeCell inside lock; chmod-555 case proves audit survives write crash
